verdicts.title: Nouns Retro Grant Proposal: France Tour 2026
verdicts.reason: This is a retroactive grant of ~$12,500 for a delivered children's art and cultural-exchange tour spreading Nouns brand and CC0 culture — well below the 100 ETH cap. Retroactive funding for visible delivered work is FOR by default (I.4), and it plainly qualifies as mission spending under I.1. Calldata (sendOrRegisterDebt to the named proposer and a collaborator) is consistent with a reimbursement grant; note a portion is framed as forward 'seed funding,' but the ask remains small and reversible per I.3.

verdicts.title: Incubating the ⌐◨-◨  Clubhouse in EUROPA
verdicts.reason: This is mission spending — funding Nouns-branded physical events, workshops, and community-building in Europe — with an ask (~43k USD) well below the 100 ETH cap, so Article I.1 defaults FOR. The proposer shows a visible track record (Haus of Nouns, Nouns Town LA, adidas collab) and disclosed returning 40k USDC of prior unspent funds, satisfying Article IV.2. The heavy operator-compensation line is a builder grant rather than self-dealing liquidity under IV.3, but the truncated calldata means the recipient address and exact transfer amount could not be fully verified against the stated USDC figure, warranting human review.

verdicts.title: The theatre that made national news without funding. Now with a payback model.
verdicts.reason: This is mission spending: a CC0-licensed theatre production featuring a Nouns character, touring to spread Nouns culture, with a $40,000 USDC ask well below the 100 ETH cap. Under Article I.1 the default is FOR, and the proposer shows a visible track record (13 years operating, a prior Shakespeare production seen by 8,000+, ongoing touring) satisfying I.2 scrutiny even if the ask exceeded the cap. The claimed 5% revenue payback is unenforceable framing, not an onchain mechanism, but it does not alter the underlying mission-spend character; we value the grant on its proliferation merits, not the promised return.

verdicts.title: Rabbit Playground: Inclusive Play for Every Child
verdicts.reason: This is mission spending: a CC0 open-source, Noun 674-inspired inclusive playground that proliferates Nouns art and culture (QR to nouns.wtf, CC0 3D designs, collaborative content). The $3,250 USDC ask is far below the 100 ETH cap, making it a cheap, low-risk experiment, and the proposer cites a visible track record (Props 483/709). Defaults FOR under Article I.1 and I.3.

verdicts.title: Make Nouns Bid Again!
verdicts.reason: The calldata pauses the auction house, sets the reserve price to 0 ETH, and unpauses — matching the prose exactly. Per Article II.4, zeroing the auction reserve restores stalled issuance and widens participation, making it mission infrastructure rather than a treasury-freeze maneuver: default FOR. Human review is still required per II.2 because the change is structural.

verdicts.title: Grant trav delegate access to the Nouns DAO X account
verdicts.reason: This is mission spending: a small $500/month, six-month media stream (~$3,000 total, far below the 100 ETH cap) to produce governance posts, art, and memes that spread Nouns culture on the DAO's X account. The proposer cites a visible track record via prior Lil Nouns-funded media work, and the ask is a cheap, reversible experiment with DUNA admins retaining root access and deletion power (Article I.3). The calldata (createStream to the recipient plus a payment registration) is consistent with the described streaming grant.

verdicts.title: Secret Ballot Voting & Sealed Bid Auctions for Nouns DAO
verdicts.reason: This funds development of secret-ballot voting and sealed-bid auctions — groundwork for changes to governance vote legibility and auction mechanics, which is structural under Article II.5 (reconnaissance for structural change) and defaults AGAINST; secret ballots reduce vote legibility, which III.2 opposes (the constitution's open question confirms III.2 stands). The onchain actions are unconditional transfers of a Noun and 111 stETH that encode none of the prose's investment terms, holding periods, vesting, milestones, or delivery commitments, so prose and calldata conflict (IV.1) and a >100 ETH lump-sum lacks enforceable milestones/streaming (I.2). Requires human review as a structural proposal.

verdicts.title: 24/7/365 Nouns Instagram @lilnouners
verdicts.reason: This funds ongoing social-media marketing that spreads Nouns and CC0 culture, a mission-spending proposal well below the 100 ETH cap (~$11,250 total at $15/hr × 15hrs × 50wks). The proposer shows a visible three-year track record maintaining the account with 10k+ monthly reach, satisfying Article I.1's default-FOR posture and I.4's credit for delivered work. The calldata is a routine debt/payment registration with no structural component.

verdicts.title: Delegate ENS tokens to Gramajo
verdicts.reason: Action 2 transfers a single treasury Noun (826) to Gramajo, a named individual with visible delivered work (led the Contract Naming Season effort earning the DAO 500 ENS tokens) — this is recognition-in-kind mission spending under Article I.5, not treasury mechanics, and one Noun is well under [CAP]. Action 1 delegates externally-held ENS governance tokens to keep them active, which is participation-positive and does not alter Nouns treasury, auction, or governance mechanics. The Noun transfer is a single named recipient not bundled with any Nouns mechanics change, so it stays under Article I rather than Article II.

verdicts.title: Resume the Proliferation: 0 ETH Reserve
verdicts.reason: The calldata pauses the auction house, sets the reserve price to 0, and unpauses — restoring stalled daily issuance and lowering the cost of joining, which Article II.4 explicitly classifies as mission infrastructure with a default FOR posture rather than treasury-freezing mechanics. Decoded actions (pause, setReservePrice(0), unpause) match the prose. As a structural change to auction mechanics, human review is still required per II.2.

verdicts.title: Route No-Bid Nouns to the Treasury NounsAuctionHouseV4 upgrade
verdicts.reason: This upgrade changes auction settlement mechanics by redirecting unsold (no-bid) Nouns to the treasury — expressly named in Article II.1 as structural, default AGAINST. The II.4 direction test does not rescue it: routing no-bid supply into the treasury does not restore stalled issuance, lower the auction reserve, or widen participation; it accumulates Nouns in the executor without any disposition policy, tending toward concentration rather than proliferation. No extraordinary justification, and no formal audit was performed. The calldata (a single upgrade call) matches the prose, but the mechanic itself carries Article II's AGAINST posture.

verdicts.title: Noggles Playground as Outdoor Therapy for Children with Autism
verdicts.reason: This is a small mission-spending ask ($3,250 USDC, far below the 100 ETH cap) funding a CC0 open-source Noggles playground design that spreads Nouns culture and serves a public-good purpose (inclusive therapy for children with autism). It is a cheap, reversible experiment with a proposer showing a visible track record (props 483 and 709), warranting the benefit of the doubt under Article I.3. The calldata registers a USDC payment to the named proposer, consistent with the prose.

verdicts.title: Add custom FREE multicolor Accessory to Nouns Collection
verdicts.reason: This proposal adds a new CC0 accessory trait to the Nouns collection at zero treasury cost, expanding the shared visual commons — squarely mission spending that proliferates Nouns art and culture under Article I.1, and a cheap, reversible contribution favored by I.3. The calldata (addAccessories on the art descriptor) matches the prose and touches the art collection, not treasury, auction, entity, or governance mechanics, so Article II does not apply.

verdicts.title: NOUNS (AND NOGGLES) AT TAIPEI TOY FESTIVAL
verdicts.reason: This is mission spending under Article I.1 — funding Nounish vinyl toys and a branded booth at the Taipei Toy Festival spreads the Nouns brand and CC0 culture to a new collector audience. At ~28,535 USDC the ask is well below the [CAP: 100 ETH], so the default is FOR and no milestone/track-record scrutiny is triggered (I.3). The unenforced profit-sharing and 'attend regardless' framing are minor concerns but do not rise to self-dealing or a calldata mismatch; the calldata pays in USDC as described.

verdicts.title: We tried crypto instead of public funding. It worked, without the money.
verdicts.reason: This is mission spending: a CC0-licensed theatre production integrating Nouns imagery and story, with a $40,000 ask well below the 100 ETH cap and a visible track record (prior Shakespeare production reaching 8,000+ people, still touring). Under Article I.1/I.2 the default is FOR given the demonstrated delivery. However, the truncated sendOrRegisterDebt calldata does not let me verify that the token and amount match the stated $40,000 ask, so the amount must be confirmed before execution.

verdicts.title: NOUNS AT TAIPEI TOY FESTIVAL
verdicts.reason: This funds art, toys, and event exhibition that spread Nouns and CC0 culture at international toy festivals — squarely mission spending under Article I.1, with a ~23,735 USDC ask well below the 100 ETH cap. The calldata uses sendOrRegisterDebt on the DAO's USDC payer, consistent with the stated USDC-denominated ask, and Article I.3 favors this kind of accessible outreach experiment.

verdicts.title: Nouns x 501c3 Study
verdicts.reason: This proposal funds a study to scope creation of a sister 501c3 entity alongside the DUNA — legal/governance groundwork for an entity-structure change. Under Article II.5, reconnaissance for structural change inherits the Article II posture (default AGAINST, never auto-ratified) even when the calldata only spends; the II.4 direction test does not save it, as forming a new charitable entity is entity-structure engineering rather than widening participation or restoring issuance. The bundled Noun transfer to the self-authoring proposer, tied to entity mechanics work, likewise stays Article II rather than qualifying as recognition-in-kind.

verdicts.title: Set Auction Reserve to 0
verdicts.reason: The calldata pauses the auction house, sets the reserve price to zero, and unpauses — exactly matching the prose. Article II.4 explicitly treats zeroing the auction reserve as mission infrastructure that restores/widens issuance, defaulting FOR rather than as a treasury-freeze. This is structural per Article II, so human review is still required regardless of the FOR verdict.

verdicts.title: Party Under the Sea
verdicts.reason: The ask is ~13,000 USDC — well below the 100 ETH cap — to fund a Nogles-shaped Biorock coral reef that spreads Nouns brand and CC0 culture through public-goods ecological art, squarely within Article I.1 default-FOR mission spending. The calldata (a USDC ERC-20 transfer) matches the prose ask, and as a small, reversible experiment with staged photo/video deliverables it earns the benefit of the doubt under I.3, despite unverified recipient naming.

verdicts.title: Boosting Nounish Art School @escueladeartenounish
verdicts.reason: This is mission spending under Article I: a community art-education program in Peru that spreads Nouns and CC0 culture, with a $67,600 ask well below the [CAP: 100 ETH]. The proposer shows a visible track record (prior Nouns Amigos funding, sold-out merch, ongoing since 2024), satisfying I.2, and the Nouns auction activation segment directly proliferates the brand. The unlabeled recipient address and unverifiable exact token/amount in the truncated calldata are minor concerns that lower confidence but do not rise to a clear calldata mismatch.

verdicts.title: Nouns × Kismet Closing Party Farcon Rome @ Vera3 Space + Live Auction
verdicts.reason: The event spend itself ($9.5k for an IRL Nouns activation) would default FOR as mission spending under Article I.1, but actions 2 and 3 transfer two treasury Nouns (#1193, #1316) to a multisig escrow to run a novel off-platform live-auction disposal at a 0.7 ETH reserve. That is not an in-kind grant to named builders under I.5 — it is a Noun transfer bundled with a new treasury/auction mechanism, which I.5 and II.1 keep in Article II with a default AGAINST posture. The direction test (II.4) is ambiguous — proceeds grow the treasury and could onboard members — so this cannot be auto-ratified and must go to human review.

verdicts.title: Set Auction Reserve Price to 2.8 ETH
verdicts.reason: The calldata sets the auction reserve price to 2.8 ETH via setReservePrice — a change to auction mechanics explicitly named in Article II.1. Under the II.4 direction test this raises the barrier to entry and restricts issuance (the freeze direction), so it defaults AGAINST rather than qualifying as mission infrastructure. As a structural proposal it can never be auto-ratified and requires human review per II.2.
